headline: Academic Staff at Public Universities 35% Contract Workers
context: One-third of academic staff at public universities are on fixed-term contracts without permanent tenure prospects.

cards.big: 35% of academic staff at 20 public universities are contract employees in 2025
cards.sub: Contract lecturers earn 30-40% less than permanent staff for identical work

cards.big: Contract academics produce 45% of published research but receive no research grants
cards.sub: Exclusion from funding pools wastes productive research capacity

cards.big: Average contract renewal cycle is 2 years creating persistent job insecurity
cards.sub: Short-term contracts discourage long-term research commitments and mentoring

cards.big: France converted 8,500 fixed-term academic positions to permanent roles in 2022
cards.sub: Tenure-track conversion improved research output 22% within 2 years

cards.big: Universities demanding world-class research from insecure staff are demanding loyalty without reciprocity
Should contract academics who perform for 3 years be automatically offered permanent positions
